title: Curanga fel-terrae
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant "Curanga fel-terrae." It is possible that this plant is a misspelling or a fictional name. However, I can suggest a similar plant called "Curanga" also known as "Curanga fel-terrae" is not found but a similar plant known as "Curanga"  (Muntingia calabura) is found in South America and is also known as Jamaican cherry or strawberry tree.

If we consider that Curanga is the name of the plant then here is a short paragraph about Curanga.

Curanga (Muntingia calabura) is a small tropical tree native to the Americas, known for its vibrant red fruits and medicinal properties. The health benefits of Curanga include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to help reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against infections. The bioactive constituents of Curanga include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Curanga include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and skin infections.
